collections and remittances; and

1.7. That the Municipal Accountant to prioritize and exert every effort to
reconcile, trace and correct the negative balances noted on the account.

2.9. We recommended that Management provide subsidiary ledgers on the
aforementioned PPE accounts and exert extra effort in reconstructing the
accounts earmarked as “Lacking Documents to Establish” with
coordination with the inventory team for physical substantiation of the
PPE.

2.10. Further, please refer to COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 20,
2020 with Subject: Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items
Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items,
for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government
Agencies, for proper and efficient treatment of the deficiency
aforementioned.

3.8 We recommended that the Management make provisions to insure vital
assets with the GSIS.

4.6. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation to direct the
Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to look for the available
documents and substantiate the balances of the Receivable and Payable
accounts. Prepare ledgers and schedules correspondingly. If it will prove
ineffective in spite of enormous effort made, the accountant and the
municipal mayor may request the write off of the receivable accounts
subject to the guidelines and the requirements set forth in COA Circular
No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. And, henceforth, submission of
FS should be accompanied with the required schedules in adherence to
the provisions of Sections 111.1, and 112 of PD 1445.

6.5. We recommended that management enact measures to enforce immediate
liquidation of the concerned officials and employees’ unliquidated cash
advances such as sending demand letters effecting immediate settlement
of the same, otherwise, necessary sanction for its settlement such as the
withholding of the salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances
may be enforced. Moreover, ensure that cash advances be liquidated in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated
February 10, 1997.

7.7. We recommended that Management transfer the unutilized prior year
balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the DRRM Special Trust
Fund pursuant to Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012 and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the
unexpended balances of LDRRMF by year of transfer, DRRMF from
other LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor in the Trust Fund books.
Also make sure the timely transfer of the funds, ideally at the beginning of
every year.

8.6. We recommended that management immediately transfer any remaining
funds to the Special Trust Fund depository bank account and take up the
cash donations under the Trust Liability-DRRMF in the Trust Fund books
in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular 2014-
002.

9.5. We recommended the Management to update the Notes to Financial
Statements submitted to the COA and effecting the requirement of
comparative presentation of data with that of the preceding year, and
henceforth, comply with the guidelines on the FS presentation as provided
in Paragraph 53 of IPSAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accounting Unit to effect the proposed adjusting journal entries:
Grants and Donations in Cash 	4-04-02-010		9,943,867.14
Due to LGUs			2-02-01-070			4,096,068.32
Due to Other NGAs		2-02-01-050			5,847,798.82
To correct erroneous recognition of income upon payment of progress billing.
Henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recognizing revenue in every payment of progress billing, and recognize the assets and income only upon the transfer of the asset to the General Fund. Likewise, encourage the Municipal Accountant to revisit the MNGAS and IPSAS for proper treatment of financial transactions.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Accounting Office to hasten the reconciliation and submit immediately the signed RPCI to the Team. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain stock card and stock ledger card, respectively, to facilitate periodic reconciliation and monitoring.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze and identify the cause/s of the abnormal balances of the above accounts and effect the necessary adjustments to establish their correct balances in order to fairly present the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
reclassify those completed projects from the CIP accounts to their appropriate PPE accounts and provide the related depreciation expense with the proposed adjusting journal entries presented in Annex F;
regularly coordinate with the Engineering Office regarding the status of the projects in the CIP accounts to determine which of those are already complete and to make the necessary reclassification thereof; and
review and account for the details of the CIP with unclear project name.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in reconciling the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable financial reports and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation to facilitate early detection of errors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accounting Unit to hasten the reconciliation and submit immediately the signed RPCPPE to the Audit Team. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile the balances of the PPE and biological asset of its respective records.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit a justification on procuring the above-mentioned project and services under the Direct Contracting modality together with the documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the Audit Team’s evaluation and validation. We further recommended that Management observe the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in procurement to ensure that prices and terms obtained are favorable to the government.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to secure the necessary supporting documents as proof that disbursements charged against the 20% development fund are in connection with the implementation of developmental projects that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU, and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to refrain from charging salaries and wages of job order personnel and other expenses that are administrative in nature under the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1 and other pertinent rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management require the concerned Accountable Officers (AO) to liquidate their cash advances and ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a demand letter to hasten the liquidation. If after the demand and no action from the AO, we further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall cause for the withholding of AO’s salary or from whatever amount due to them.

We recommended and the Management agreed to procure the common-use supplies and equipment from the Cebu Regional Depot of PS-DBM in accordance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011, Administrative Order No. 17 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184. We also recommend on the compliance with the documentary requirements stated in GPPB Resolution No. 17-2020 for those CSE items which are not available in the PS-DBM at the time of procurement before pursuing the procurement from other sources.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, adopt and implement cost measures to observe economy in the incurrence of telephone and internet expenses, such as:
Review the amounts of communication allowance taking into account the available landlines and internet connection, and consider its reduction;
Utilize alternative modes of communication such as the internet for long-distance communication and evaluate the features of postpaid plans of the communication networks on its charging for services to other network that provides cost saving measures.
We also recommended that if cell card/e-load shall be allowed, consider purchasing in bulk by the GSO, following the required procurement procedures and distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Administrator submit explanation/justification on the use of two postpaid plans, for the Audit Team’s evaluation.
Furthermore, submit the official receipts to support the expenditure, and statement of accounts for those granted under postpaid plan and proof/evidence that those were incurred for official functions.

We recommended that Management to: a) Provide or add the Accounting Office and knowledgeable and dependable staff to help with the reconciliation works, and b) The Accounting Office to prepare and submit, regularly, to COA without delay the bank reconciliation statements of all its bank depository accounts maintained at the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), Surigao City Branch.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit  strictly observe with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.

Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC be updated always of the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting the GPPB website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.
We recommended that the Management stop the utilization of the Bayanihan grant for purposes other than for the implementation of COVID-19 PPAs and expenses to prevent future disallowance.

We recommended that the Management submit the lacking documents mentioned such as the Sales Invoice, BAC Resolutions and Requisition and Issue Slips.  Henceforth,   the Bids and Awards Committee should conduct public bidding or any applicable alternative mode of procurement in compliance with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit the original copies of Reports of Checks Issued so that validity of reported cancelled checks could be verified and evaluated by this office.  Adherence to the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual and other prescribed regulations is enjoined.
We recommended that the management submit the lacking supporting documents mentioned to establish validity of the claims and prevent suspensions in audit.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer utilize the prescribed format in Annexes A and B together with the specific guidelines in the preparation of LDRRM Investment Plan and Utilization provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommended that the LDRRMFIP includes not only the current year appropriation but also the utilization of unexpended balances in the trust fund in order to maximize its utilization for projects and activities that are geared towards disaster- preparedness, prevention and mitigation.

We recommended that the Management prepare the required summary/list of donations in-kind received, distributed and balances supported with Acknowledgement Receipts, Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries and Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, and submit to the accounting unit for proper recording and monitoring, copy-furnished the audit team.  The one-time separate report for cash and in-kind donations shall also be prepared and submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD and accordingly post the required report in the NDRRMC and the agency website.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit a copy of the contract indicating the warranty clause and the duly filled-up Omnibus Sworn Statement (for procurement ₱500,000.00 and above) as shown in Appendix 1 of the Circular to support its undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act. Henceforth, the BAC should be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.
We recommended that Management comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR to ensure enforcement of the provisions of the Safe Spaces Act.

We recommended that Management: a) Provide the Accounting Office knowledgeable and dependable staff to handle the reconciliation works and assist the accountant, and b) The Accounting Office prepare and submit regularly to COA without delay the bank reconciliation statements of all its bank depository accounts maintained at the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), Surigao City Branch.
We recommended that Management provide updated property numbers for these equipment.
We recommended that the Management instruct the property officer to issue proper documents upon issuance of the PPE or semi-expandable items as aforementioned.

We recommended that the Management direct the concerned accountable officers to accomplish the necessary report/s and/or procedure in property disposal as provided in the Manual on Property and Supply Management and use the reports (IIRUP) as the basis of the de-recognition of the property from the inventory.

We recommended that the Management create an inventory committee to do works as provided in the Manual on Property and Supply Management System; and submit the Reports on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2020 and adhere to the prescribed date of its submission for CY 2021 and succeeding years and reconcile these reports with the accounting records.
We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers and schedules for each PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Strengthen the GAD Focal Point System of the LGU by (1) providing enough proficient personnel to support the Focal Person.
Require the Focal Person together with her team to: (a) develop and maintain GAD database containing gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data, gathered systematically, among the LGU population and clients; (b) regularly update these data; (c) conduct gender analysis on these data for planning, programming and policy formulation; (d) implement the identified PPAs; and (e) closely monitor the progress of activities to meet the objectives and realize the performance indicators and targets.

Moreover, the Management should ensure that the gender issues are clearly stated in the GAD Plan and design and formulate its PPAs according to the specific identified gender issues to address and resolve the same, supported with valid analyses.

We recommended that the Management: a) Prepare and submit the monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format as shown in Annex C of the foregoing LBC; b) post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month; and c) send a written notice when said reports have been posted on its website to above mentioned government agencies.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to remit immediately to the BIR the withheld taxes balance as of December 31, 2020 in the amount of ₱150,135.42 to avoid incurring penalties and surcharges; and Adhere strictly to Section 4.114.C of Revenue Regulation (RR) No. 2-98 in the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee accomplish properly the RPCPPE after the physical count so that information on the existence, status, condition and location of PPE items can be established.
Further, we recommended that management commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU. The persons liable for unaccounted properties should be determined for appropriate legal action, if warranted.

We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to carefully analyze the accounts with abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Chief Executive direct and ensure that the Accounting unit to ensure that trial balances accounts have updated subsidiary ledgers; and submit the schedules duly supported with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the ten (10) percent retention during progress payments for infrastructure projects in accordance with the Contract Implementation Guidelines for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects.
We recommended that Management submit an explanation/justification on why the variance of ₱4,349,971.74 should not be disallowed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the appropriate taxes, if any, to be imposed on the collection of Canyoneering Activity Fees or the taxes to be withheld on the share of income to other stakeholders in accordance with the National Internal Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code to increase the taxes and other fees pursuant to Sections 129 and 191 of RA 7160 in order to have additional revenue sources to fund the projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that the concerned Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately subject records, disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports due for submission to avoid disallowance in audit.  It is further recommended that payment of salaries be suspended for those officials/employees who failed to submit reports within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management submit a written explanation on why the Annual Procurement Plan for 2019 is inconsistent with the duly approved Annual Budget for the same period despite previous years’ observation and also not in the prescribed form.  Henceforth, preparation of APP should be consistent with the Annual Budget and in accordance with RA No. 9184 and related issuances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and submit to COA without delay the CYs 2016-2019 BRS of all its bank depository accounts maintained at the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), Surigao City Branch and to comply always such requirement mentioned in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert more effort to look for the available documents and substantiate the balances of the Accounts Receivable, Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Due from Other Funds accounts.  Prepare ledgers and schedules correspondingly.  If it will prove ineffective in spite of enormous effort made, the accountant and the municipal mayor may request the write off of the said receivable accounts subject to the guidelines and the requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.  Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445 so that the accounts in the financial statements can be readily verified to their existence, accuracy and reliability.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed, through the coordination of the Municipal Engineer, implement all the PAPs included in the 20% DF budget. Optimally utilize the fund as planned.  Every PAPs included in the AIP was considered very important, extremely necessary and imperatively urgent.  As such, there will be no reason that these may not be given priority to be implemented during the year; The Municipal Treasurer also agreed to ensure transfer of cash equivalent to 20% of the IRA to the 20% DF bank account; Stop the charging of non-developmental expenses against the 20% DF.  Submit to COA the legal basis of the payments totaling P521,627.21.  Non-compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted legal basis or proof and the same was found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; and adhere faithfully to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160, and Items 3, 4 and 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the utilization and implementation of the 20% DF.

We recommended that Management stop the charging of administrative/ operating expenses against the LDRRMF, as well as those disbursements without corresponding appropriation. Submit to COA the legal basis on the payment of office equipment, travel expenses for non-DRRM related activities, communication, internet expense and fuel for non-disaster related activities in the total amount of P138,501.70 and those expenditures without appropriation amounting to P97,560.20. Non-compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; Direct the MDRRMO-designate to include in the LDRRM Plan the allocation for the Response, Rehabilitation and Recovery areas of the 70% allocation as mentioned in Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2013-1; Require the Municipal Accountant to accrue and transfer the unutilized/unexpended LDRRMF to the special trust fund and to maintain subsidiary ledgers by year of transfer; Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account to be used as a special trust fund for the unspent/unutilized LDRRMF; Advise the MDRRMO-designate to prepare and submit to COA the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445, Items 4 and 6.4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and Item 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on budgeting, utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Chief Executive ensure that the inventory
committee faithfully discharge their duties and functions and conduct complete
physical count of property, plant and equipment, submit the report thereon to the
concerned auditor and reconcile with accounting records any discrepancies found.
Also, ensure that the Accounting unit be manned by adequate personnel to meet
deadlines and submit complete and timely reports specifically supported with the
required schedules
Otherwise, cause the suspension of payment of salaries of personnel
concerned pursuant to Section 122 (2) of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that management submit the lacking documents without
delay and justify other noted deficiencies to avoid disallowance.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on why the
variance of ₱274,987.55 should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management conduct competitive bidding or any
alternative mode when justified by conditions in the procurement of catering
services for its various activities following the guidelines prescribed in GPPB
Resolution No. 12-2017.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation on the
circumstances surrounding the hiring of the private lawyer and why it should not be
disallowed.

We recommended that Management through the BAC submit a written
explanation on why the procedures for procurement of consultancy contract
involving individual consultant was not observed.

We recommended that Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment be
prepared for issuances of non-expendable properties and equipment to end-users to
record the accountabilities of the end-users.

We recommended that Management classify the canyoneering operation as a
separate economic enterprise through an ordinance by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Accounting Unit maintain a
special account in the general fund for canyoneering operations as well as other
economic enterprises operated by the LGU.

We recommended that the Management submit its APP/Revised or
Supplemental APP for 2018 as consolidated by the BAC or resolutions of its
Sanggunian authorizing its different procurement as ruled by the Supreme Court in
the case of Quisumbing v. Garcia to avoid the disallowance of all procurement.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipality adhere to the
provisions of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of
Republic Act 9184 mandating that all government procurement shall be in
accordance with the Annual Procurement Plan of the procuring entity to avoid the
disallowance of its procurement transactions.

1.5 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their books to settle the variance.
Reconciliation must be done at least quarterly as prescribed in Section 181(c)
of the GAAM, Volume I. The reconciliation includes the verification of the
cash accounts that had zero balances against the ledgers in the Accounting
Office and information from the bank to establish whether these accounts are
indeed closed; verify the cash account that had a negative balance with the
ledger and/or the bank statement to ascertain an amount which could be
used as a beginning balance in the cashbook for a certain period/cut-off date;
and adhere to Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 181(c) of the GAAM to
present reliable cash balances in the financial statements.

2.6 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and submit to COA without delay the CYs 2016-2018 BRS of all its
bank depository accounts maintained at the Land Bank of the Philippines
(LBP), Surigao City Branch and to comply always such requirement
mentioned in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA
Circular No. 96-011.

3.6 We recommended that Management direct the Property
Custodian/Inventory Team to ensure that the balances in the RPCI and
RPCPPE should reconcile with the amounts reflected in the ledgers of the
accounting. Make sure likewise that the needed information in the Reports
like the cost/book value of the supply or property, date it was acquired
estimated useful life and location and condition of the asset shall be provided
therein. Advise the Municipal Accountant to assist the Property
Custodian/Inventory Team in the said reconciliation; advise the Municipal
Mayor to constitute an inventory committee to spearhead the planning and
conduct of inventory of the supplies and properties of the LGU. This way,
proper physical count thereof, correct preparation of the Reports and prompt
submission of the same to COA will be made; instruct the Property
Custodian/Inventory Team to submit to COA the RPCI and RPCPPE on or
before January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
for LGU; advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and
other properties Ledger Cards. Reconcile the accounting records with the
list/reports being kept by the Property Custodian at least quarterly; instruct
the Property Custodian to tag every PPE with property number and
description. This will help in easy and fast monitoring of the assets and
identification thereof in case a loss of the same. The corresponding ARE/PAR
shall also be made and issued by the Property Custodian to the end user. These
ARE/PAR should be completely kept, maintained and monitored; and adhere
to Sections 114, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other
pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count
of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the
Auditor.

4.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books for the
total borrowing cost incurred in CY 2018 in the amount of P2,136,501.53
which should be capitalized and will form part of the asset by debiting the
appropriate asset account and crediting Prior Period Adjustment. This
amount should likewise be presented or disclosed in the Notes to Financial
Statements as borrowing costs capitalized during the period and the
capitalization rate used to determine the same; and adhere to Section 112 of
P.D. No. 1445 and the provisions of Items 18 and 40 of PPSAS No.5 on the
correct recognition and disclosure of the borrowing costs in the financial
statements.

5.5 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the LDRRMO
to allocate 30% QRF separate and distinct from the 70% allocation in the 5%
LDRRMF. This will serve as a standby fund integrating its PAPs in the
LDRRMFIP. Inform the MPDO that Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC
No. 2013-1 requires the allocation of the 30% and 70% separate and
distinct from each other; stop charging administrative/operating expenses
against the LDRRMF. Submit to COA the legal basis on the payment of
meals, communication and internet expenses and other non-DRRM related
travel expense in the total amount of P25,924.00. Non-compliance hereof, or
after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was
found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the
amount as circumstances may warrant; direct the MDRRMO to exclude from
the LDRRMFIP the illegitimate PAPs as mentioned in Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-
DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; instruct the Municipal Treasurer to open a
separate bank account to be used as a special trust fund for the
unspent/unutilized LDRRMF. The bank account utilized for TF-General can
be used as a recipient of the unspent/unutilized LDRRMF but a separate
subsidiary ledger for the unspent/unutilized LDRRMF should be prepared and
maintained; direct the Municipal Accountant to accrue and transfer the
unutilized/unexpended LDRRMF to the special trust fund and to record such
transfer in the respective books. The procedure was duly prescribed in Item
5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. The same COA Circular mandates the
Accountant in Item 5.1.12 thereof to maintain subsidiary ledgers by year of
transfer; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No.
10121, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Item 4.3 of
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and Items 5.1.5, 5.1.10 and
5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the budgeting, utilization and
reporting of the LDRRMF.

6.5 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to transfer cash equivalent to 20% of the IRA to the 20% DF bank account;
stop charging non-developmental expenses against the 20% DF. In addition to
the legal basis of the payments totaling P312,525.00, submit to COA the
program of work (POW) and/or activity design of each of the PAPs, or any
document showing that every foregoing-mentioned expense is integral in
implementing thereof or bringing these projects or programs into ready use.
Non-compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted legal
basis or proofs and the same was found wanting of merit, this may result to
suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant;
include only in the AIP the PAPs that contribute to the attainment of desirable
socio-economic development and environmental management as provided in
Section 3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; optimally utilize the fund as
planned. Every PAPs included in the AIP was considered very important,
extremely necessary and imperatively urgent. As such, there will be no reason
that these may not be given priority to be implemented during the year; and
adhere faithfully to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160,
and Items 3, 4 and 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the budgeting and
utilization of the 20% DF.

7.5 We recommended and the Local School Board agreed to plan, consider
and include in the budget only PPAs which are among those considered
priority expenditures under the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1 s. 2017 and within the tenet enshrined in Section 4(7) of P.D.
1445. Repairs or maintenance of administrative rooms or buildings as well as
those expenses incurred for teachers and administrators’ trainings and
seminars, either for academics and other purposes, can be funded from the
general sources of the Department. Thus, excluded as legit expenses of the
SEF.

8.6 We recommended that Management direct the MSWDO to implement
ALL the PAPs under the LCPC. The PAPs included in the annual LCPC
budget are prioritized and considered important. Thus, the MSWDO should
find means to implement the same and not be overlooked; recheck and
monitor each of the PAPs vis-à-vis the corresponding target beneficiaries to
avoid mislooked realization of the objectives; PAPs that were initiated by other
government agency for the LGU’s implementation should be consistently
followed up from the former to ensure realization thereof; payments to
implement the LCPC PAPs should be charged against its specific fund unless
the disbursement can be attributed to other appropriate operating expense
account; stop charging from the LCPC fund the expenditures that do not
directly benefit the children and the youth. Submit to COA the legal
basis/justification on the payments of non-LCPC expenses amounting to
P34,470.00. Non-compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this end of the
submitted legal basis/justification and the same was found wanting of merit,
this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances
may warrant; and adhere faithfully to Sections 2 and 15 of R.A. No. 9344 and
Section II of DILG MC No. 2012-120 on the budgeting and utilization of the
LCPC fund.

9.5 We recommended that Management appropriate an amount for GAD
PAPs at least 5% of the total annual budget. The allocation thereof should not
be based from IRA; direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based
on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived
from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections
4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of JMC No. 2013-01 and to create and maintain a GAD
database which will be used to identify prevalent gender issues present in the
Municipality; submit to the COA Audit Team a copy of the GPB and AR in a
manner prescribed in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly
adhere to the governing provisions set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the creation, utilization
and reporting of GAD fund.

10.5 We recommended that Management reprimand and direct the
concerned officials and employees to immediately submit the respective
Reports, Statements, and other documents to COA in a manner prescribed in
Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Chapter 4 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

11.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from or
exercise prudence in incurring meals and snacks during meetings, session and
hearing to be responsive to the needs of the constituents and to ensure that
government resources are used equitably and wisely; submit to COA the
lacking documents stated in the Annex A herein attached; furnish COA the
justification and/or legal basis of paying the previous years’ meals and/or
snacks using CY 2018 appropriation amounting to P126,511.74. Failure to
comply, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the
same was found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or
disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; provide the Audit
Team the justification and/or legal basis on the procurement of meals and/or
snacks through reimbursement scheme totaling P183,863.74. Again, non-
compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this Office and the same was found
wanting of merit, this will cause the issuance of suspension or disallowance as
circumstances may warrant; afford the Audit Team documents/proof that the
visit of the Mayor to Cebu, Iligan City, Cagayan de Oro, Butuan City or
Surigao City where payments for meals and/or snacks with visitors had been
incurred, were official and duly authorized by the Governor; and ensure
adherence to Sections 4(1), 4(6), and 119 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 10 of
R.A. No. 9184 in the payments of meals and/or snacks.

12.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of
procuring goods through reimbursement scheme. Provide the Audit Team the
justification and/or legal basis on the procurements made through
reimbursement in the amount of P337,677.19 Failure to comply, or after
evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was found
wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount
as circumstances may warrant; refrain from utilizing current year
appropriation to pay previous years’ obligations. Furnish COA the
justification and/or legal basis of reimbursing previous years’ expenses totaling
P75,432.30 using the CY 2018 budget. Again, non-compliance hereof, or after
evaluation by this Office and the same was found wanting of merit, this will
cause the issuance of suspension or disallowance as circumstances may
warrant; support the disbursements with complete documents to establish
existence, validity and propriety of the payments. Submit to COA the lacking
documents stated in the Annex A herein attached. Also, the supporting
documents must be completely filled up to provide clear information of the
transaction. The same also must be signed by the authorized LGU officials
and/or employees; and ensure adherence to Section 10 of R.A. No. 9184 and
Sections 4(1), 4(6), and 119 of P.D. No. 1445 in the procurement of various
goods of the LGU.

13.5 We recommended that Management submit to COA the
justification/comment on the demolition of the public market building without
the approval/consent of the Commission. Non-compliance hereof, or after
evaluation by this end of the submitted explanation and the same was found
wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount of
P230,328.49 or any amount that may apprised soon by independent government
agency, as circumstances may warrant; and ensure compliance with Sections 2
and 79 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 379 of R.A. No. 7160 and Items B and C of
COA Circular No. 89-296 when to demolish/condemn government property.

14.2 We recommended and the Management agreed to immediately remit
the outstanding tax delinquencies to avoid payment of penalties and
surcharges.

15.2 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the
concerned persons responsible or liable to settle their audit suspensions or
disallowances in accordance with the Revised RRSA.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the Project Brief and the MOA. Follow up from time to time the grant of the said financial assistance from the source agency so that the start-up capital will be immediately available and the project will commence its operation; find ways, with the assistance of the municipal engineer, to maintain and to prevent the building from further deterioration. Keep the door always closed and locked so that the people cannot get inside and will have access for destruction. No. 1445 in the implementation of various PAPs of the LGU so that losses and wastages of the government resources could be avoided; and submit to COA the Project Proposal and the MOA covering the implementation of the project.

No. 9184 and Item I of LBC No. 109 in the implementation of LGSF projects and other PAPs of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the balances. Identify the items that
need adjustment and record them appropriately in the affected books.

We recommend that the concerned Accountant/Bookkeeper be directed to
submit the subject Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the written
explanation on the delayed submission thereof.

We recommend that management commence the process of disposal of
unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA
Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are
only those assets used in the operation of the LGU.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to prepare and
submit the subsidiary schedules supporting the general ledger balances and to
maintain updated subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with
complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.

It is also recommended that management review its personnel complement
and ensure that offices are manned by sufficient and competent personnel to meet
deadlines and submit complete reports.

We recommend that management eventually fill-up the vacant positions for
five Revenue Collection Clerks to solve the problem on shortage of regularly
appointed Collecting Officers. It is also recommended that the function on revenue
collection be assigned to permanent employees properly bonded with the Bureau of
the Treasury and refrain from designating Job Order workers or casual personnel
in the revenue collection department. Further, adequate safeguards to protect the
funds of the municipality should be instituted.

We recommend that management submit to the concerned auditor the basis
for the imposition of entrance and users fees to tourists visiting Kanlaob River and
experiencing canyoneering adventure in the place.

We recommend that management submit a valid explanation on why
observers were not invited, failure to post PO in required media and some
deficiencies noted in the supporting documents specifically the absence of
specifications/details of items ordered.

We recommend that management submit a written explanation on why
subject procurement opportunities were not posted in the PhilGEPS website and at
any conspicuous place reserved for the purpose in the premises of the procuring
entity. It is also recommended that the BAC and other personnel involved in
procurement update themselves on the latest Handbook on Philippine Government
Procurement, its 2016 Revised IRR and latest GPPB Issuances.

We recommend that management cause the submission of the basis for the
grant of 8% of the net proceeds of canyoneering operation to Compostela Framers
Association as well as its accreditation documents as required in COA Circular No.
2007-001.

We recommend that management submit a valid explanation on why they
proceeded with the procurement of the service despite absence of the required BAC
action, contract of service and the Specific Implementation Agreement.

We recommend that management submit a valid justification/explanation for the
absence of required BAC action for the above procurement and other
deficiencies noted. Henceforth, concerned officials are enjoined to observe the
proper procurement procedures outlined in RA 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR for
any procurement activity of the LGU.

We recommend that the BAC submit a valid explanation on why the
advertisement in PhilGEPS website for the procurement of online booking software
for ecotourism failed to include details of software specifications and return on
invested capital which are vital information to make the advertisement appealing to
prospective bidders and achieve its purpose.

We recommend that management direct the BAC to choose a mode of
sending the invitation to observers keeping in mind the requirement that the
invitation should be received by the observers five days before the procurement
activity.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to coordinate with the Municipal
School Board (MSB) and communicate with DepEd Alegria District and
request for the immediate liquidation of cash advances granted to DepEd
officials and employees;
b) Require the MSB to authorize the municipal treasurer to disburse funds
from the SEF pursuant to the budget prepared and in accordance with
existing rules and regulations and discontinue the practice of granting cash
advances to unauthorized individuals; and
c) Recommend for write-off all unsettled cash advances which had been in the
books for over ten (10) years in accordance with the guidelines set forth
under COA Circular 2016-005 on the Guidelines and Procedures on the
Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances and
Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local
Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled
Corporations (GOCCs).

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to review the
computation of the unexpended/unobligated balance of DRRMF as of December 31,
2017 and transfer the same to a Special Trust Fund account in accordance with
Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to be used solely for DRRM programs
and activities.

We recommend that management distribute the remaining manuals to
identified recipients so the same could be utilized and served its purpose and
effectively achieve the expected response of the constituents in times of emergency
and calamity.

We recommend that management eventually hire qualified personnel once
financial resources allow to complete the mandated positions in the DRRM Office in
order that the functions and responsibilities thereof be discharged in accordance
with Joint Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommend that management allocate at least 1% of its IRA, as a
separate budget for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects
and activities advocating child rights separate from its allocation for other social
services and use the fund pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120
dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend that management furnish the concerned auditor with the
GAD AR within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding
year as required in item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

1.5	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify immediately the collections totaling P5,912,559.88 which were considered cash shortages from the Cash-Local Treasury to the appropriate Receivable accounts; and advice the Accountant to provide detailed and comprehensible narrative explanation in the Notes to FS on the collections totaling P490,807.82.  These will make the accounts in the FS fairly presented.

2.6	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to effect the required reconciliation and to perform the same regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I,; instruct the Municipal Treasurer to make and maintain cashbooks for all the accounts of the LGU as required in Sections 26 and 27 of NGAS for LGU, Volume II; and adhere to Sections 111 and 181(c) of P.D. 1445 and GAAM, respectively, as well as Sections 26 and 27 of NGAS for LGU, Volume II to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

3.6	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to COA without delay the CYs 2016-2017 BRS of all its bank depository accounts maintained at the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), Surigao City Branch and to comply always such requirement mentioned in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

4.6	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to COA clear, comprehensible and detailed ledgers and schedules supporting the balances of the accounts in the FS.  These ledgers and schedules shall be the source of information to facilitate verification of the accounts in the FS. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445 so that the accounts in the FS can be readily verified to its existence, accuracy and reliability.

5.7	We recommended that Management designate a GSO in accordance with the provisions of Article 20 of R.A. 7160; Constitute an inventory committee/team.  The team will spearhead in planning and conduct of physical count of the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team Report on the Physical Count thereof pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Ledger Cards; Advise the Storekeeper to maintain Property Cards per category of Inventories, PPE and Biological Assets and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records regularly; and adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

6.5	We recommended that Management optimally utilize the fund through the implementation of its PAPs as planned; direct the LDRRMO to allocate 30% QRF separate and distinct from the 70% allocation in the 5% LDRRMF. This will serve as a standby fund integrating its PAPs in the LDRRMFIP. Inform the MPDO that Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 requires the allocation of the 30% and 70% separate and distinct from each other; transfer to a special trust fund the unspent LDRRMF, except capital outlay, in a manner and purpose stated in Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121; disclose in the Notes to FS the amount and other details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF as prescribed in Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and as shown in the sample form provided in Annex D thereof; advise the Accountant to prepare and certify the monthly and annual reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the LDRRMO to submit the same to COA in a manner prescribed in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the creation, utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.

7.5	We recommended that Management include in the LCPC budget only the PAPs authorized under Section II of DILG M.C. No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. Submit to COA the legal basis on the payment of clothing allowance amounting to P17,000.00. Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; ensure inclusion in the budget only the PAPs that are considered priority and realizable during the year; and adhere faithfully to Sections 2 and 15 of R.A. No. 9344 and Section II of DILG M.C. No. 2012-120 on the budgeting and utilization of the LCPC fund.

8.5	We recommended that Management stop the practice of granting CAs for special purposes to persons who are not employees of the Municipality, or were not appointed nor designated as disbursing officer of the LGU. The Municipal Treasurer may draw the CA for the purpose of granting financial assistance to Ms. Tan for implementation of various PAPs; avoid granting of additional CAs unless the previous ones had been liquidated and proper accounting thereof was made; advise Ms. Tan to liquidate immediately her CA pertaining to the reading encampment activity which was granted to her under check number 2681623; remind the Accountant to always issue a certification of no outstanding unliquidated CA which shall be attached to a CA. This will serve as an integral document and a requirement before an additional CA be granted to a particular accountable officer; and strictly adhere to Sections 89 and 101(2) of P.D. No. 1445, Section 99(b) of R.A. No. 7160 and Items 4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and reporting of CAs.

9.5	We recommended that Management encourage every barangay or cluster of barangays to establish an MRF where sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and reusable waste materials will take place. Subject to the availability of funds by the LGU, appropriate financial assistance to the barangays for the construction of MRFs therein; collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes from the barangays in compliance with Section 10 of the Act. The collected types of materials and wastes could be segregated and disposed in a manner the LGU is presently treating the kind of wastes collected from other sources other than the barangays; advise the heads or any person in authority of the market, schools and municipal buildings regarding the requirements for the segregation and storage of solid waste as stated in Section 22 of the Republic Act; revisit the provisions of the ESWMP and identify the problems which precluded the implementation of the programs. If it’s still feasible, implement the activities without delay. If in negative, amend the ESWMP in a manner prescribed in the Plan or other issuances so that only doable and prioritized projects will be included in the ESWMP; and ensure compliance to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specially Sections 10, 22, 32 and 33 thereof on the segregation, collection and disposal of solid waste materials.

10.5	We recommended that Management seek an SB ordinance/resolution allowing the LGU to hire JOs and to incur obligations as a result of availing their services; submit to COA the legal bases on the hiring of JOs and payments of their wages. Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; and adhere to the requirements stated in Section 77 of R.A. No. 7160 as regards the hiring of JOs and payments of wages thereof.

11.5	We recommended that Management reprimand and direct the concerned officials and employees to immediately submit the respective reports and other documents to COA in a manner prescribed in Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Chapter 4 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

12.5	We recommended that Management remind the SDO/Mayor that the cash advances for confidential fund shall be limited to the requirements for three (3) months and that the DV covering the said cash advance shall clearly state the duration of implementation of the projects; submit to COA all the lacking documents supporting the cash advance as mentioned above in compliance with Sections 6.1.7 and 6.1.8 of the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01; instruct the municipal accountant to submit to COA ATL, in a sealed envelope, the cash advance voucher together with the supporting documents pertaining to the cash advance of confidential fund in a manner prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of the Joint Circular; demand from the SDO/Mayor the liquidation of the cash advance for CF and to submit to ICFAU for audit the liquidation thereof; and adhere to Sections 6.1.5, 6.1.7, 6.1.8, 6.1.8.4, 6.2.1 and 6.2.2.7 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances for CF.

13.5	We recommended that Management stop the transfer of funds from SEF to GF and TF. Section 9 of R.A. no. 5447 provides no exception thereof; discontinue the transfer of cash from TF to other funds except for the reason of fulfilling the specific purpose for which it was created or the funds received; stop the practice of diverting funds through ADA without the covering DVs and supporting documents. Transactions that are duly covered with DVs and supported with complete documents assured proper approval by the authorized municipal officials and the validity and propriety of the same will be established; submit to COA all the JEVS pertaining to the recording in the books all the fund transfers through ADA; and adhere strictly to Sections 4(3), 4(5), 4(6) and 84(2) of P.D. No. 1445, Section 9 of R.A. No. 5447 and LBC No. 112 covering the inter-account and inter-fund cash transfers.

14.6	We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; formulate and include only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; implement the GAD activity or activities that will resolve GAD issues and concerns; submit to COA a copy of the formulated and duly approved GAD Codes or ordinances; submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB in prescribed form and in a manner stated in Section 4.1.C.6.2 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC and the accomplishment report stated in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation, utilization and reporting of GAD fund.
15.2	We recommended that Management immediately remit the outstanding tax delinquency on VAT and E-VAT to avoid payment of penalties and surcharges.
16.2	We recommended that Management require the concerned persons responsible or liable to settle their audit suspensions or disallowances in accordance with the Revised RRSA.

17.6	We recommended that Management appropriate an amount through a supplemental budget or realignment/augmentation to acquire the restaurant.  In appropriating the amount, consideration must be given on the sources of the revenue or savings which shall be the basis for the preparation of the supplemental and realignment of budget, respectively.  This changes in the annual budget shall be authorized by the Sanggunian; request authorization from the Sanggunian for the Mayor to enter into contract with the Vice-Mayor if there is a budget constraint.  The Agreement must provide among others that the Restaurant shall remit to the LGU reasonable share from its sales to commensurate the resources provided by the Agency to the restaurant; stop the payments of the salaries to the JO who was assigned in the restaurant as well as its utility expenses.  Public monies shall be used for public purpose; and never to allow anybody to construct, improve, modify or alter any public property without the consent of the authorized LGU Officials.  This authority must ensure that the transactions were also covered with an SB Ordinance granting such and Agreements/Contracts.

18.6	We recommended that Management conduct/make a feasibility or project study for the LAMSTAC and to every income-generating unit maintained and to be established/created by the Municipality to determine the social and economic viability of the enterprise; inform the Sanggunian that SB Resolutions establishing or creating economic enterprises should contain specific, detailed and comprehensive operating guidelines/procedures governing the operations.  These guidelines should serve as a framework if addendums will be made therefrom.  The procedures that cover matters from the point the clients applied for the services offered by the unit, conduct of services, billing, collection, recording and monitoring should be printed and compiled for information, guidance and easy reference; prepare and maintain comprehensive financial records for information, reference and monitoring; and revisit the guidelines on billing and collections.  Identify and note the reasons of delayed payments.  Impose stricter measures in the recoupment of the receivables.

19.6	We recommended that Management come to an agreement with the provider of the accounting system and prepare a MOA relative to the transaction which shall be executed by both contracting parties.  A copy of the agreement shall be furnished to COA within five (5) days from its execution; direct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the system and identify the causes of the delay or non-preparation of the financial data.  Rectify such causes.  Consult and seek assistance from the provider/supplier if needed.  Timely prepare and submit to COA the financial statements of the LGU together with the journals, ledgers, journal entry vouchers and other schedules appurtenant thereto in a manner prescribed in the existing laws, rules and regulations; and adhere faithfully to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 so that the interest of the government shall always be upheld in any agreements made with the other parties and that the objectives of every program, projects and activities of the LGU will be achieved.

20.6	We recommended that Management revisit the provisions of the project proposal and/or MOA.  Identify the cause/s of the delay in the grant of the 2nd tranche of the financial assistance from the DA like the non-compliance by the LGU to certain requirements for its release.  Rectify/comply the cause/s or the requirements without delay. Follow up from time to time the grant of the said financial assistance from the source agency so that the start-up capital will be immediately available and the project will commence its operation; find ways, with the assistance of the municipal engineer, to maintain and to prevent the building from further deterioration.  Keep the door always closed and locked so that the people cannot get inside and will have access for destruction. Reconsider the provisions in the project proposal and/or MOA about obligation and accountability and utilize/avail the same if there is any.  Restoration/repair costs should always be answered by the party at fault, as provided in the Agreement; adhere to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of various PAPs of the LGU so that losses and wastages of the government resources could be avoided; and submit to COA the Project Proposal and the MOA covering the implementation of the project.

21.6	We recommended that Management instruct the MAO to implement without delay the projects covered by the 2nd batch of the financial assistance from the DA; advise the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer who have the first hand information of any financial assistance transferred to the LGU to promptly inform the concerned unit/end user in the municipality so that immediate action/implementation on the matter will be done.  The MPDO is likewise enjoined to monitor the implementation of various PAPs of the LGU; submit to COA the project proposal and the MOA covering the implementation of each of the mentioned projects; and be always reminded/guided on the objectives of the programs and faithfully adhere to the provision of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various BUB projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage.

22.6	We recommended that Management initiate another intensive dialogue with the contractor and find doable and effective solutions to the problem.  If it is still feasible to finish the project, request the contractor to do so.  If it’s not, discuss the matter with the contractor and the DILG on the possible remedies to take in accordance with the applicable provisions in the Contract with due consideration of incurring no loss on the part of the government; advise the contractor to file a Suspension of Work before the Management. This will avoid the imposition of liquidated damages for every unjustifiable day of delay.  Execute liquidated damages if there is non-compliance thereof.  Terminate the contract with the MIGO Construction if the provision on contract rescission/termination in Section 68 of R.A. No. 9184 will warrant.  Rescission/termination of contract will result also to forfeiture of performance security; and adhere to Section 2 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, Section 68 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and Item I of LBC No. 109 in the implementation of LGSF projects and other PAPs of the LGU.

23.6	We recommended that Management revisit the Project Brief and the MOA of the program.  Remind the association on the objective of the project and advise them to follow/do all the activities to be undertaken that were stated therein to achieve it.  These activities include the proper maintenance of operating machines so that the production to be done to meet the target income in a month, quarter, semester or year would not be hampered, proper recording and keeping of financial transactions and the required reporting thereof, among others; advise the municipal employee concerned like the Accountant, Treasurer or the MPDO to regularly monitor the condition and performance of the projects.  Monitoring includes ocular inspection, confirming compliance to the provisions in the study brief and agreements and requiring submission of financial reports.  These ensure achievement of the project objective as well as guarantee profitability and sustainability of the same; and submit to COA the Project Brief/Study covering the implementation of the project.

24.6	We recommended that Management revisit the provisions of the project proposal and/or MOA.  Identify the cause/s of the delay in the grant of the 2nd tranche of the financial assistance from the Province of SDN like the non-compliance by the LGU to certain requirements for its release.  Rectify/comply the cause/s or the requirements. Follow up from time to time the grant of the said financial assistance from the source agency so that the construction of the traveler’s lounge phase will be started immediately; find ways, with the assistance of the municipal engineer, to maintain and to prevent the constructed comfort rooms from further deterioration.  Reconsider the provisions in the project proposal and/or MOA about obligation and accountability and utilize/avail the same if there is any.  Restoration/repair costs should always be answered by the party at fault, as provided in the Agreement; adhere to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of various PAPs of the LGU so that losses and wastages of the government resources could be avoided; and submit to COA the Project Proposal and the MOA covering the implementation of the project.

25.6	We recommended that Management implement without delay the aforementioned projects.  Instruct the BAC to revisit the bidding process and identify the causes on non-identification of the winning bidder for the construction of the covered court.  If the comment from the prospective bidders holds true, advise the TWG to revisit and re-evaluate the Detailed Engineering Design (DED) together with the Program of Work (POW).  If the amount of P100,000.00 would not be enough to completely construct the project, compute the needed amount to finish it and try to request additional funding from the Provincial Government of Surigao del Norte to meet the required sum.  The computation must be made in consideration with the given DED and POW.  If not viable or if the request was denied, utilize the available amount to construct the court.  Reconstruct the DED and the POW if necessary with due concern with the purpose thereof; advise the municipal accountant to furnish COA the ledger detailing the budget and expenses of the project construction of municipal children playground; submit to COA the project study and the MOA covering the implementation of the projects; and adhere to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the LGU’s various projects, programs and activities with a view that the resources of the government must be safeguarded against loss or wastage.
