observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned chief executive cause the immediate
submission of said BRS and institute appropriate sanctions on employees
responsible for the delayed submission/non-submission, if warranted on the basis of
the evidences/documents.

1.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the accounts with the subject amount from Cash-Local Treasury to
the appropriate Receivable accounts to present the same fairly in the financial
statements.

2.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
exert more effort to conduct bank reconciliation by using the December 31,
2016 cash balance per bank statement as the unadjusted beginning balance
and to work back taking into consideration the reconciling items until both
the bank and the book balances reconcile. Prepare and submit BRSs of all
the bank accounts maintained by the LGU in the manner prescribed by
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular 96-011.

3.8 We recommended that Management: (1) Designate a GSO in accordance
with the provisions of Article 20 of R.A. 7160; (2) Constitute an inventory
committee/team. The team will spearhead in planning and conduct of physical
count of the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit
Team Report on the Physical Count thereof pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS
Manual for LGU; (3) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property
Ledger Cards; (4) Advise the Storekeeper to maintain Property Cards per
category of Inventories, PPE and Biological Assets and to reconcile balances
thereof with that of the accounting records regularly; (5) Adhere to Sections 119,
120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and
regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance
of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

3.9 The Management committed to comply with the recommendations and to
create an inventory committee to lead the conduct of physical count.

4.6 We recommended that Management: (1) Appropriate fund to construct
a secured shelter where to store these unserviceable properties awaiting for
disposal; (2) Create a disposal committee who will spearhead in planning and
implementing disposal procedures in accordance with the existing guidelines of
disposing government properties; (3) Dispose all unserviceable properties per
recommendation of the disposal committee for added income of the LGU and to
give room/space of the garage where to park municipal vehicles; (4) Inspect and
evaluate from time to time whether these private vehicles in the municipal
garage are occupying for an official business. Private vehicles without legit
purpose should be returned to the prospective owner. The space vacated shall
be utilized for municipal vehicles only and for vehicles which will avail MCES
services. Municipal vehicles should be always parked in the covered garage
when not in use; (5) Strictly observe Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in utilizing
government scarce resources.

4.7 Management concurred with the audit recommendations and explained that
some of the serviceable properties are due to be transferred to another location where
it will be utilized.

5.5 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and detailed schedules of the
financial accounts of the LGU to provide adequate information regarding the
account balances. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 111(1), 112 and
114 of P.D. No. 1445.

6.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
identify the reconciling items of the affected reciprocal accounts and effect the
adjustments thereof in the respective books to present fairly the said accounts
in the financial statements.

6.6 The Municipal Accountant, gave his assurance with the compliance of the
audit recommendation.

7.5 We recommended that Management: (1) Submit to the Auditor the
missing DVs shown in the schedule. Failure to comply may cause the issuance
of Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance for the subject amount of the
vouchers; (2) Reports should be complete and comprehensive. List of checks
issued should include the nature of payments, among others; and (2) Adhere to
Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 44 of NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Volume I in the submission of DVs and supporting documents to the
Auditor.

7.6 The Municipal Treasurer said that he will see to it that all the disbursement
vouchers will be submitted.

8.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and maintain separate books of accounts as well as the financial
statements/reports for each of the economic enterprises created and maintained
by the LGU as prescribed in Section 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume
I. The financial reports will provide vital and sufficient information to the end-
users as regards the assets, liabilities and equity of each of the economic
enterprises and to draw decisions therefrom.

8.6 The Municipal Accountant is amenable with the audit recommendations and
will prepare the required reports.

9.5 We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to
maintain cashbooks for each economic enterprise of the municipality wherein to
record the receipts and disbursements of the entities. Adhere to Section 313 of
R.A. 7160 in the creation and maintenance of special accounts in the general
fund as well as the proper recording of the transactions appurtenant thereto.

9.6 Management commented that they will require the maintenance of the
cashbooks and ensure compliance with the audit recommendation.

10.5 We recommended that Management: (1) Immediately remit to the
government collecting agencies the withheld contributions and loan payments;
(2) Stop the practice of utilizing current payroll deductions to pay previous
month’s unremitted contributions and loan amortizations; and (3) Adhere strictly
the provisions embodied in Section 6(b) of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 8291, Rule
VII Section 3(a) of the IRR of R.A. No. 9679, and Philhealth Circular No. 01
s.2014 in the schedule of remittance of the amounts withheld from payroll.

11.5 We recommended that Management: create a plantilla for GSO or
designate an employee to the position; encourage the GSO to practice stocking
of a three (3)-month supplies so that immediate needs of the Agency will be
addressed; advise the BAC to prepare the Abstract of Quotation and forward
the same to the GSO for the preparation of the PO; instruct the GSO to prepare
the PO with proper and complete information and have it conformed with the
supplier. Remind the GSO to tell the supplier that delivery of the supplies
procured must be made direct to the GSO and in accordance with the terms and
conditions stated in the PO; require the Inspectorate Team to inspect the items
delivered and prepare IAR if found in order. Otherwise, return the goods to the
supplier if found defective. The IAR must be given to the GSO for its signature
and furnish it, together with the PO and delivery receipt to the Accounting for
recording. It may be allowed that the end-user will prepare the ObR and DV
but its preparation must be made after the delivery of the goods and the
information therein must be based from the PO, IAR and delivery receipt;
inform the GSO that the received supplies must be properly kept, segregated
and tagged and issuance thereof to the end-user must be recorded in a manner
provided in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; and faithfully adhere to
Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I in resolving other matters
relating to procurement of supplies and materials.

11.6 Management commented that they had difficulty in following the proper
process because of the absence of a GSO. They agreed to consider hiring or
designating a GSO to adhere the recommendations.

12.6 We recommended that Management immediately call the attention of the
department heads concerned to timely prepare and post to the bulletin boards
allotted for the purpose the required documents/reports enumerated in DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134 in a manner prescribed in the provisions
thereof.

12.7 The Management committed to comply with the recommendations and to post
the needed reports in the respective bulletin boards.

13.6 We recommended that Management: (1) Prepare and issue a well-defined set
of guidelines/policy governing the implementation of AICS program which
must be aligned with the provisions of DSWD Administrative Orders No. 5 and
1 s. 2008 and 2011, respectively; (2) Prepare a determinable appropriation
specifically for the AICS program; (3) Devolve to the MSWD the assessment of
the beneficiaries and determination of the grants to be given under the program;
(4) All applicants must be required to submit documents to establish
identification of the claimants and the validity and legality of the claims.
Supporting documents to the disbursements must be complete and duly filled
up; (5) Advise the MSWD that keeping of records on AICS program is
paramount as one of the standard operating procedures in government
transactions; (6) Set up a revolving fund to expedite delivery of service. Custody
of such fund must be assigned to the Municipal Treasury; and (7) Faithfully
adhere to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of any
program of the LGU.

13.7 The MSWDO explained that the program has long been implemented and
admitted that the guidelines currently followed needs to be polished. She assured
compliance with the audit recommendations.

14.6 We recommended that Management: (1) Revisit and revise the existing
guidelines/policies governing Eskolaran Program to have clearer and more
comprehensive parameters for its implementation; (2) Adhere faithfully to the
guidelines set for the program; and (3) Observe Section 447(5)(xi) of R.A. No.
7160 and Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of the Eskolaran
program.

14.7 Management commented that they will refine the existing guidelines for the
Eskolaran program and consider the recommendation of providing additional check
and balance to protect the fund from misuse.

15.7 We recommended that Management: (1) Stop utilizing the Annex
Livelihood and Development Center as offices for Municipal Accounting,
Budget and Treasury. The structure should be used as intended and for the
purpose of the 20% DF. Administrative offices should be transferred to any
available spaces in an administrative building; and (2) Adhere to Section 2 of
P.D. No. 1445 and Section 2.3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 in the utilization
of the 20% DF.

15.8 Management admitted that the annex livelihood and development center
building is currently utilized for administrative purposes and is considering to transfer
the administrative offices to another building to comply with the audit
recommendations.

16.5 We recommend that Management: (1) Use the fund optimally through
the implementation of the PPAs stated in the CY 2016 Annual Work and
Financial Plan of the Municipality; (2) Transfer to a special trust fund the
unspent LDRRMF, except capital outlay, for the purpose stated in Section 21 of
R.A. No. 10121; (3) Disclose in the Notes to FS the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF as prescribed in Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and as
shown in the sample form provided in Annex D thereof; and (4) Advise the Local
DRRM Officer and the Accountant to prepare and submit monthly reports on
Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA in a manner prescribed in Item
5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

16.6 The Municipal Accountant commented that they will open a new bank
account where the unspent LDRRMF will be deposited and will disclose the needed
information in the subsequent financial statements.

17.6 We recommended that Management: appropriate at least 5% of the total
annual budget for GAD PAPs as prescribed by the JMC; formulate and include
only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote
equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which
specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; categorically include in the
establishment of the GFPS the Executive Committee, Technical Working Group
and/or Secretariat. These are responsible in carrying the functions of the Point
System; set up and maintain the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-
responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; carefully plan,
identify and consider gender issues and concerns in the LGU that these may be
included in the GPB, with due consideration that optimal utilization of the
appropriated amount for the program will be achieved; submit to the concerned
government oversight agencies the GPB in prescribed form and in a manner
stated in Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the provisions
set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the
creation and utilization of GAD fund.

17.7 During exit conference the GAD focal person claimed that the 2016 GAD
GPB was already reviewed and approved by the DILG and that she was not able to
provide the GFPS and Executive committee to the Auditor because she failed to
secure a copy of the said document. Further, she assured compliance with the audit
recommendations.

20.2 We recommended that Management require the concerned persons liable to
settle their disallowances in accordance with the Revised RRSA.

observations.recommendation:

Otherwise, the Commission on Audit will have to submit the reports of unliquidated cash advances, in aggregate amounts of P50,000.00 and above per accountable officer, to the Civil Service Commission, Philippine Anti-Graft Commission, Office of the Ombudsman or Department of Justice for the filing of appropriate charges
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant is instructed not to allow additional cash advance to those employees with outstanding cash advances

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management take effective and efficient tax collection efforts and develop strategies to improve the collection of taxes. observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging expenses to 20% DF which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/ capital expenditures so that development programs and projects envisioned by the government are properly implemented. Direct the Municipal Officials to strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in JMC No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: Prioritize projects that are included in the DILG – DBM JMC 2011-01
observations.reason: Some non-development projects have been stopped. Non-development projects were still charged against the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management open a separate bank account, as Special Trust Fund, for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to support disaster risk reduction management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council within the next five years. Require the Municipal Accountant to record all unexpended/ unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the DRRMF-MOOE and transfer the same to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund” (Code 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books. observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop paying wages of non-teaching personnel using Special Education Fund as it is not authorized in Republic Act No. 5447 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000. Moreover, direct the Local School Board to prepare Annual School Budget Board within the aforementioned law and circular. Likewise, direct the Disbursing Officer to submit complete documentary requirements in the granting and liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Otherwise, require the Municipal Accountant not to allow the Disbursing Officer for another cash advance and payments of claims without complete required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and prepare PPMP and consolidated APP. Require the BAC to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated PPMP and APP yearly for us to validate and ensure that all procurement undertaken by the LGU were based on the approved annual procurement plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly adhere Section 10 of R.A. No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations by conducting public bidding for procurement of goods and infrastructure projects. Require the BAC Secretariat to submit the complete contract documents as provided in RA 9184 either the programs/projects were undertaken by contract or by administration. Moreover, file appropriate charges against responsible employees, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: All projects will undergo public bidding except if the conditions for alternative modes is met
observations.reason: Not all procurement of projects were supported with documents; procurement of medicines were done without public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere faithfully the provisions cited in the Memorandum of Agreement, program of work and Work and Financial Plan. No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Development and Planning Coordinator, Local School Board, Inventory Committee and Property Officer to submit the required reports immediately. Instruct the Municipal officials and employees concerned to adhere strictly the prescribed period in the submission of reports as provided in COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Sections 50, 51, 56 and 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Otherwise, the continued failure of the officials concerned of the submission of the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of his salary until he shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials are directed to submit the required reports on time
observations.reason: Only the GAD accomplishment report was submitted to date all other reports were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation:
1) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) daily and forward the same to the Accounting Office together with the disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents.
2) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents within first ten (10) days of the ensuing month in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
3) Reprimand all accountable officers concerned for the immediate submission of all the RCIs and disbursements for all funds to the Auditor. Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government obligations is a ground for administrative disciplinary action and disallowance of the transactions as provided in Section 39 (1 &3) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Besides, failure to render accounts for a period of two months after such accounts should be rendered is penalized in Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: All DVs will be submitted to COA
observations.reason: Delay was because some of the DVs were not yet submitted by the treasury to the accounting office within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to adhere strictly the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002 and furnish the Auditor of the copies of contracts, purchase orders and its supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection. Moreover, require the BAC Secretariat to submit all contracts and POs together with its complete required supporting documents within the prescribed period for contract review. Direct the Municipal Engineer to request the Auditor for technical inspection on completed projects and submit required documents. Otherwise, we recommend that appropriate sanctions as provided in Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 be imposed to responsible officials/employees concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive as Head of the Agency to ensure the proper granting and utilization of all cash advances as provided in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002. The accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate and/ or settle their overdue outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, such failure / or settle the same shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of their salaries and the instruction of other sanctions. The Municipal Accountant to regularly update the Monthly Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances of Accountable Officers including cash advances for salaries, wages and other specific purpose and show its complete details for every cash advance and its liquidation. Submit the same on or before 5th day of the following month. Besides, require him to attach a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant is instructed not to allow additional cash advance to those employees with outstanding cash advances
observations.reason: Majority of the cash advances remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the guidelines set forth in Joint Memorandum Circular of the DBM and DILG No. 2011-1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization. Moreover, require the Municipal Budget Officer to evaluate whether the transaction is one of the priority development projects and programs embodied in the AIP. Otherwise, it is deemed to be without appropriation. Additionally, stop the practice of charging expenses to 20% Development Fund which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/ capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Charging of expenses to 20% DF which are not related to development projects and projects were stopped
observations.reason: Some non-development projects have been stopped. Non-development projects were still charged against the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to stop processing claims against government funds without complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and adhere strictly COA Circular No. 2012-001 which prescribes the revised guidelines and documentary requirements for common government transactions.
observations.managementAction: Lacking documents has been complied with. Some disbursements still had deficiencies in supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some disbursements still had deficiencies in supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Designate Municipal Treasurer to maintain cashbooks in all accounts and record the financial transactions of the Municipality daily pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, direct him to update and balance all the transactions and reconcile the same with the cash on hand as provided in Section 63 (D) of LTOM. Moreover, direct the Liquidating Officer to maintain Liquidating Officer’s Cashbook for each fund and shall be updated and balanced daily as provided for in Section 63 (B) of LTOM. Besides, compel all the Revenue Collectors to maintain Teller’s/ Collector’s to record all collections received and all remittances to the designated Liquidating Officer in accordance with Section 63 (C) of LTOM.
observations.managementAction: Accountable Officers in the treasury office to maintain and update their cashbooks
observations.reason: Revenue collectors does not maintain individual cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of splitting the purchases because it is strictly prohibited by law. Base the procurement on Program of Work to avoid splitting of transactions and use appropriate mode of procurement. Besides, conduct competitive public bidding for all procurement which does not fall under Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 pursuant to Section 10 of the same IRR. Otherwise, direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare recommendation for approval of the LCE to use alternative methods of procurement. Further, require the BAC to review the purchase request, request for quotation and purchase orders if completely and properly filled up before award to the lowest bidder is made. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to check and review the completeness of the supporting documents and of the required information therein.
observations.managementAction: The practice of splitting of contracts will be stopped by management
observations.reason: Splitting of contracts were still observed during CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) daily and forward the same to the Accounting Office together with the disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to submit the same to Auditor concerned within first ten (10) days of the ensuing month in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-006. Besides, reprimand all accountable officers concerned for the immediate submission of all the RCIs and its supporting documents to the Auditor. Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government obligations is a ground for administrative disciplinary action and disallowance of the transactions as provided in Section 39 (1 &3) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Also, failure to render accounts for a period of two months after such accounts should be rendered is penalized in Article218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: All DVs will be submitted to COA
observations.reason: Delay was because some of the DVs were not yet submitted by the treasury to the accounting office within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management call the attention of the Municipal Accountant on the non-submission of the trial balances and year-end financial statements. Moreover, require him to submit the financial reports together with its complete supporting schedules within the time frame set by law, rules and regulations to avoid sanctions by law.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is directed to comply with the requirements
observations.reason: No Trial Balance for CY 2016 were submitted to the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Auditor concerned with copies of program of works and detailed estimates, plans and specifications and accomplishment reports on infrastructure projects even undertaken by administration for technical review and final inspection as the case maybe. Moreover, direct the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders together with its supporting documents within 5 days from the execution/issuance as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001. Besides, require the Inspectorate Team to furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of delivered goods and services pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-002. Further, advise the Municipal Accountant to stop processing claims against government funds without complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: All projects will be provided with program of works.
observations.reason: Some projects were not provided with program of works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the taxes due to BIR to avoid paying surcharges, interest and penalties arising from violations. Moreover, we recommended that Municipality implement strictly the policies on the prompt remittance of taxes withheld in compliance with BIR Regulations No. 2-98.
observations.managementAction: For adjustment by the Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: Not all taxes were remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the Annual GPB using the prescribed form and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, submit it to the Provincial Planning and Development Office then to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval. She develops GAD programs, activities and projects (PAPs) to strengthen the Municipality’s gender-responsive governance and ensure that GAD Fund is utilized in align with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01. Moreover, she prepares the annual GAD Accomplishment Report based on the DILG-approved GPB or the enacted GPB following the form prescribed in Annex E.
observations.managementAction: The GAD FP claimed that the GAD PB was submitted to the DILG for review and approval but no copy was furnished to the Office of the Auditor.
